name: rad-session
description:
**Make Claude Code sessions remember each other — even across machines.** rad-session manages the lifecycle of a Claude Code project: one-time setup, per-session orientation, per-session capture, automatic git sync across machines. The default Claude Code experience loses session state at context compaction, lets CLAUDE.md grow forever, and forgets everything when you switch machines. rad-session fixes those three failures.

What each command does:
- **`/init`** (once per project) — Detects your tech stack via pure-Python scripts (no LLM guessing), recommends which other rad-* plugins fit, scaffolds CLAUDE.md (merging strategic `@-imports` from rad-planner's CLAUDE-FRAGMENT if present), creates `.claude/session-log.md`, runs a plugin-bloat audit that proposes per-project `enabledPlugins` disables to cut token cost, ensures session files aren't gitignored. Safe to re-run.
- **`/startup`** (every session start) — Fetches origin and prompts to pull when behind *before* reading any handoff file (so you're never silently reading stale state after switching machines). Gap-checks the 5 strategic docs (PRD / ARCHITECTURE / ASSUMPTIONS / DECISIONS / PLAN) and soft-warns if missing. Reads HANDOFF.md + session log. Discovers project resources (MCPs, CLIs, env template) with mtime caching so repeat runs are nearly free. Presents a concise briefing including a "Continuing from <other-host>" note if the last session was on a different machine.
- **`/wrapup`** (every session end) — Tags the conversation for DECISION / FAIL / CORRECTION / INSIGHT / OPEN labels (recency-bounded — long sessions don't scale wrapup cost). Writes a structured HANDOFF.md with a "What NOT To Do" field (TRIED / FAILED BECAUSE / CORRECT APPROACH so the next session knows which dead ends to avoid). Derives a compressed session-log entry mechanically from the handoff (no second LLM synthesis pass). Optionally prompts to append tagged decisions to DECISIONS.md as sequence-numbered, append-only entries. Prunes CLAUDE.md if it changed this session, with diff confirmation (the `## Resources` section is protected). Auto-commits session files and prompts to push.
- **`/add-resource`** (any time) — Register a new MCP server, CLI tool, script, or note to CLAUDE.md's Resources section so `/startup` picks it up next session.

**Cross-machine continuity** is the headline feature. `/wrapup` auto-commits HANDOFF.md + `.claude/session-log.md`; `/startup` fetches and prompts to pull before reading them. PC ↔ GitHub ↔ Laptop stays in sync without manual git work. Flag overrides for autonomous loops or specific workflows: `--push` / `--no-push` on wrapup, `--auto-pull` / `--no-pull` on startup, `--quick` for short sessions.

**Safety:** Never `git add -A`. Never force-pushes. Never merges or rebases automatically. Bails cleanly on diverged history. The `## Resources` section in CLAUDE.md is protected from automatic prune. PreCompact hook fires *before* context compaction so state isn't silently lost when Claude Code starts summarizing.

Four Python scripts back the lifecycle: `detect-stack.py` (deterministic tech-stack detection from lockfiles + marker files), `detect-resources.py` (MCP + CLI discovery with PATH verification and mtime-cached results), `audit-plugin-bloat.py` (per-project plugin relevance audit, cuts token cost on projects that don't use a given plugin's stack), `migrate-to-v4.py` (one-shot upgrade for projects on the pre-3.0 / pre-4.0 layout).

Pairs naturally with rad-planner — rad-session owns the session tier (CLAUDE.md, HANDOFF.md, session-log); rad-planner owns the strategic and operational tier (PRD, ARCHITECTURE, ASSUMPTIONS, DECISIONS, PLAN). Single-writer rule prevents collision. Works fine without rad-planner — `/init` falls through to placeholder `@-imports`, `/startup` soft-warns about missing strategic docs.
